mechanism: Fiscal dominance dynamics (6-7% GDP deficits, $2T+ issuance, no consolidation path) constrain central bank degrees of freedom. CB control over the correlation regime degrades as fiscal dominance intensifies — the CB can influence powerfully when fiscal policy is supportive but loses control at high debt/GDP without consolidation.
regime_dependence: The constraint binding depends on debt/GDP level and trajectory. At low debt with fiscal surplus, CB is fully unconstrained. At current US levels (~125% debt/GDP, 6-7% deficits), CB influence is partially impaired.
